text: I have always loved Coach and have had great experiences with the brand, which is why I was really disappointed by my experience with the Coach House Flagship Store.  About a month before a recent flagship-exclusive release, I went into the store and was told by an employee that she added me to a customer list specifically for that collection. I was never contacted about the release, and when I tried to follow up using the number I was given, it turned out to be a general store text line rather than a direct contact.  A few days before the drop, I called the store to ask whether the collection would actually be available in-store or if it would be fully allocated in advance, as had happened with a previous release. I explained that I need advance notice to coordinate transportation due to a disability. In response, I was told that “they have elevators,” which did not address my situation, as my disability is neurodevelopmental and not related to physical accessibility. I was also told that the items would likely be “all sold out” because they are given to people on a list first, and that there would be “no point” in coming in. Based on that information, I did not arrange transportation for the release.  This was especially confusing because I had previously been told in-store that I had been added to the list for this exact release, but I was never contacted and had no way to confirm my status.  During the release itself, I called to try to purchase an item remotely through pay-by-link due to my disability and was told the store would not process the order without an in-person visit. At that point, the only alternative offered was to try purchasing during the initial drop window, which took place around 3–4am. Due to my neurodevelopmental disability, staying up overnight is not feasible, which further limited my ability to participate.  In that same interaction, I was not offered any other accessible purchasing options. I later learned that the collection did have in-store inventory and that pay-by-link purchases were not being offered, which meant there was no accessible way for me to participate in the release.  Overall, I was very disappointed by the lack of consistent communication and accessible purchasing options. The conflicting information I received directly affected my ability to plan and participate, and I hope the flagship considers how its release and purchasing policies impact customers who cannot physically come into the store.
address: 685 5th Ave, New York, NY 10022, United States

text: This was a frustrating experience, which is disappointing as I've always had positive interactions with Coach.  I purchased the Quilted Pillow Tabby 26 in Flower Pink during my visit, but was only informed after completing the transaction that the bag I was sold was the display item and only one in stock. Prior to checkout, this was not clearly disclosed.  I was also encouraged to monogram the bag before knowing this, which would have made it final sale. Thankfully I declined, but it didn't feel transparent.  I completely understand that display items may need to be sold, especially in high-traffic stores, but customers should be informed upfront and given the opportunity to make that decision before paying, especially at this price point. No adjustment or accommodation was offered despite the circumstances.  Overall, this experience felt rushed and lacked transparency, which isn't what I expect from Coach.

text: We are long-term Kate Spade shoppers, specifically at this store. Normally, the service is wonderful, but today we had a major issue with the staff.  We went in today, March 15th (around 3PM-4PM), and saw the Stash Denim Floral Small Carryall Bag. The sign had said $129 with an additional 25% off—the sign was with the bag of the same design, but brown. We asked an associate at the store how much it was, and she said it was 25% off, and even calculated the price and showed us $96.75. After that, we agreed to check out and went to the cashier (a different employee), who also said it was 25%. We ended up paying $124 for the purse, which we realized was weird after we left the store. Not only that, but the receipt only said 10% off.  We returned to the store, where we asked about why it only showed 10% off instead of the 25% said by the sign and the two employees. The associate who calculated the price earlier was confused since she also thought it was 25%; she checked their system and said, “Oh, it was only for the brown one,” but then she asked all the staff who were behind her, and they said that the 25% off was only for the tote bags. The associate apologized and showed us the receipt, saying, “At least you still received 10% off.” No one else came to apologize or even offered a refund.  We left the store again, but ended up returning to the store for a refund because we did not appreciate their service. The person who started refunding the bag for us did not apologize and said “Mistakes can happen” multiple times after we brought up that multiple employees said 25%. Another employee came over to finish the refund, who we believe to be the manager or supervisor for this shift, gave us an attitude and barely even said a word to us.  We are very disappointed by the service of this store, especially after buying AT LEAST 10 purses from them in the past couple of years.
address: 8111 Concord Mills Boulevard Suite 266, Concord, NC 28027

text: My wife and I went to your Kate Spade store in Mebane, NC Tanger Outlet on 05/31/2026 and tried to purchase some sneakers (Ks Drift Sneaker, Silver/Captain Navy) that were clearly marked with a price of $159.20 on the bottom of the sole. But when the sales associate scanned them, they rang up at $228.00. The sales associate initially said the sale price was only for handbags. But I grabbed the sneaker myself and showed her that the $159.20 sale price was clearly marked on the sneaker itself. This proved that the pricing on the merchandise wasn't just for handbags and made things even more confusing The associate said she couldn't honor the marked sale price even though it was right there on the sneakers. She first offered a 20% discount off the $228.00 price, but when I brought up my military discount, the 20% discount became 10%. . As a loyal and frequent customer of both your retail and outlet stores, I am very disappointed. I think customers should be able to trust the prices marked on merchandise and that pricing mistakes from administrative errors should be fixed in favor of the customer.
address: 4000 Arrowhead Blvd #726, Mebane, NC 27302

text: Pathetic service  I had a really frustrating experience trying to return a bag my husband bought me as a small gift. The bag was in PERFECT condition, with all tags attached, and I was returning it immediately after opening it because I decided I didn’t want it.  The associate at the counter immediately pointed out that the dust bag was missing. I honestly didn’t even know Coach bags came with dust bags, and neither did my husband. The Gilroy store never gave him one.  Even after I explained this multiple times, she kept inspecting the bag and repeating that the dust bag was missing. The bag still even had the stuffed paper inside because i never took it out lol.  Then she called her manager, who had an incredibly arrogant and condescending tone the entire time. Instead of listening, she spoke to me like she was teaching me a lesson or giving me advice, telling me I should “check for dust bags every time I buy from them.” The audacity honestly shocked me. Coach failed to provide it, yet somehow I was being blamed for it.  The whole interaction felt accusatory and rude over something so stupidly minor. I came in for a simple return of an unused item and left feeling talked down to.  Completely unnecessary and horrible customer service
address: 234 Great Mall Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035

text: Salinas から更に北に向かいSanJoseサンホセの街に近いアウトレットです。  ここはSan Joseから少し外れた位置にありコンピュータIT関連で有名なsilicon valleyシリコンバレーと隣接した位置にある大きなショッピングモールの中にコーチのアウトレットもあり雨の日でも買い物が出来ていいのですが、どうせなら駐車場も外じゃ無ければ助かるのにと思います。  外からどこにCOACH 店舗に近く車を止めれるか見極め出来まぜん。  Olives レストランが駐車場🅿️から見えるので、このレストランを目印に駐車するとCOACH に近いです。  フードコートに日本食もあります。味は別としてお米が食べれるのはホッとします。  ここから近くに有名な大学スタンフォードがあり、その近くに友達が住んでいるのも、ここを訪れる一つの理由です。
address: 234 Great Mall Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035
